evals.prompt: Write the PR description for my latest contribution. I'm merging LittleCoinCoin/Hatch:dev into CrackingShells/Hatch:dev for v0.8.3-dev.1. Changes: (1) Added Zed editor MCP host support — reads ~/.config/zed/settings.json, supports command/args/env fields, new ZedAdapter class with validate_filtered() pipeline, new ZedHostStrategy. (2) Fixed a bug where `hatch mcp list` showed stale cache data even after the --refresh flag was passed — root cause was the refresh flag not being forwarded to the cache manager, added regression test. (3) Updated the adding-mcp-hosts skill SKILL.md to include Zed as a worked example.
evals.expected_output: A well-structured GitHub PR body with [v0.8.3-dev.1] title, a ## Summary bullet list, and detailed ## Features / ## Bug Fixes / ## Developer Experience sections with technical implementation details, following the Type A PR template.

assertions.name: title-includes-version
assertions.description: Title includes the exact version string [v0.8.3-dev.1]

assertions.name: has-summary-section
assertions.description: Body includes a ## Summary section with bullet points

assertions.name: has-features-section
assertions.description: Body includes a ## Features section for Zed support

assertions.name: has-bug-fixes-section
assertions.description: Body includes a ## Bug Fixes section for the cache bug

assertions.name: mentions-validate-filtered
assertions.description: Technical detail about validate_filtered() pipeline is mentioned

assertions.name: mentions-root-cause
assertions.description: Bug fix section explains the root cause

assertions.name: no-invented-changes
assertions.description: Does not mention features not in the provided change list (e.g. no OpenCode, no Augment, no Mistral)

evals.prompt: Draft the PR description for merging CrackingShells/Hatch:dev into CrackingShells/Hatch:main for the v0.8.3 stable release. It rolls up two dev PRs: #53 which added Zed editor MCP host support, and #54 which fixed a stale cache bug in `hatch mcp list` and updated the adding-mcp-hosts skill. No breaking changes.
evals.expected_output: A Type B (dev→main) PR body with [v0.8.3] in the title, an overview paragraph, feature sections cross-referencing (#53) and (#54), and no Breaking Changes section since there are none.

assertions.name: no-breaking-changes-section
assertions.description: No ## Breaking Changes section since there are none

assertions.name: has-overview-or-features
assertions.description: Has an Overview paragraph or Features section

evals.prompt: Write the Discord announcement for v0.8.3-dev.1. Two things landed: Zed editor MCP host support (users can now manage Zed's MCP servers with hatch mcp just like any other host), and a fix for the stale cache issue in hatch mcp list when using --refresh. Cheerful tone.
evals.expected_output: A Discord dev announcement message (Sub-type D2 style since it's a subsequent dev) using the dark-bg Hatch custom emoji, ## header, ### subsections for each change, friendly closing, no installation command required since it's brief.

assertions.name: uses-hatch-custom-emoji
assertions.description: Uses the correct custom Discord emoji (dark-bg variant for dev releases)

assertions.name: mentions-version
assertions.description: Mentions v0.8.3-dev.1 or v0.8.3-dev in the header

assertions.name: has-friendly-closing
assertions.description: Has a friendly closing line (hatching emoji or similar)
